calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Vacated

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compe Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Deft'S And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft'S And There Atorney Of Record
Off Calendar Jul-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances on THU-JUL-25-2013:
Ashley A. Ernst, Esq. of Sommers Employment Law Group, P.C. [415.432.4372] appearing for ARIEL ROSE, plaintiff / moving party;
No appearance by or on behalf of the defendant(s).

The Court adopts its tenative ruling. Plaintiff ARIEL ROSE'S Motion to Compel Further Responses to Interrogatories and Production of Documents from Defendants and Request for Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants and There (sic) Attorney of Record is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows:

As to Local Mission Eatery: Grant as to Request for Production no. 7, Form Interrogatory nos. 4.1, 214.1 Denied as to Special Interrogatory nos. 5, 10, 12, Form Interrogatory nos. 12.1, 12.2, 215.1. For Request for Production no. 7, the insurance policy must be produced. For Special Interrogatory no. 5, this is essential a request to identify ESI. Defendant is only obligated to state the form in which intends to produce the information. There is no statutory obligation to Bates stamp. For Special Interrogatory no. 10, the documents are described in sufficient detail to be used as a basis for a deposition subpoena or request for production. For Form Interrogatory no. 12.1, the term "incident" is too vague in the context of this interrogatory which is more applicable to an auto accident than it is to a series of events. For Form Interrogatory no. 12.2, Plaintiff is not prejudiced where nine possible witnesses are listed. Here, revealing which of the witnesses was interviewed compromises attorney work product.

As to the individual defendant Griffin: Granted as to Special Interrogatory no. 3, Form Interrogatory no. 4.1, 214.1. Denied as to Form Interrogatory no. 12.1, 12.2, 214.1, 215.1. Special Interrogatory no. 3 is granted subject to the parties meeting and conferring to better define the relevant documents.
Sanctions: Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is denied. There is no written record of when settlement discussions reached an impasse, and the declarations of counsel are contradictory as to that date. If, as alleged, settlement discussions lasted through July 3, then this motion was premature. Defendants seem to have reasonably complied with discovery requests and substantively responded to meet and confer letters in a not untimely manner. A large majority of the items granted above have only to do with the production of the insurance policy, but defendants have previously identified the coverage limits of that policy and answered other policy questions except the policy number.

Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)

Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
